CONTENT.Club career: Amade made his professional debut for TSG Hoffenheim in the Bundesliga on 2 March 2019, coming on as a substitute in the 82nd minute for Nadiem Amiri in the match against Eintracht Frankfurt, which finished as a 2–3 away loss.
CONTENT.External links: Alfons Amade – UEFA competition record (archive)Alfons Amade at DFB (also available in German)Alfons Amade at kicker (in German)Alfons Amade at Soccerway
CONTENT.International career: Born in Germany, Amade is of Mozambican descent. He is a former youth international for Germany.In December 2023, Amade was called up by the Mozambique national team for the 2023 Africa Cup of Nations.Scores and results list Mozambique's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Amade goal.

SUMMARY: Alfons Antonio Chico Amade (born 12 November 1999) is a professional footballer who plays as a right-back for Challenger Pro League club Oostende. Born in Germany, he represents the Mozambique national team.
